target.name: Atom Bodies
target.founder: Tyler Hayes, Doug Satzger, Eric Monsef

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Atom Bodies is a Series A-stage biotech company led by Tyler Hayes, Doug Satzger, and Eric Monsef, having raised $2M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including Series B peers such as Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression, using the $2M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implies $4M. Peer-Median anchoring across the six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $805M, with a range of $100M to $3.3B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied; while the Biotech & Health sector median P/S multiple across three public tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) is 6.6x, Atom Bodies' revenue is not on file.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $3M to $926M, with a median of $404M. This wide range reflects the significant dispersion in the peer set and the limited clarity on Atom Bodies' revenue trajectory and clinical stage. The peer-median method dominates the analysis but should be weighted against the target's relative maturity within Series A and its specific technology risk profile.
